name: Brute Force Approach
intuition_and_algorithm:
We are aiming to assign a rank to each element in the array based on how many unique elements are smaller than it. For each element, we iterate through the rest of the array, collect all smaller elements, and count the number of distinct ones.


 Initialize an empty list to store final ranks Loop over the array from index 0 to n-1 For each element:  Initialize an empty set Loop over the array again from 0 to n-1 If another element is less than the current, insert into the set (to keep only unique ones) The size of the set gives the number of unique elements smaller than current Rank = size of set + 1 Append rank to result   Return or print the rank list

complexities.time: O(NÂ²) ,Because for each element (N times), we iterate the full array again (N), making it NÂ² overall.
complexities.space: O(N) , Each iteration uses a set for unique elements which in worst case can hold up to N elements. Also, final result array uses O(N).

code.python:
class Solution:
    # Function to replace elements with their rank
    def replaceWithRank(self, arr):
        result = []

        # Loop through each element
        for i in range(len(arr)):
            # Set to hold unique smaller elements
            smaller = set()

            # Compare with every other element
            for j in range(len(arr)):
                if arr[j] < arr[i]:
                    smaller.add(arr[j])

            # Rank = unique smaller count + 1
            rank = len(smaller) + 1
            result.append(rank)

        return result

# Driver code
arr = [20, 15, 26, 2, 98, 6]
sol = Solution()
print(sol.replaceWithRank(arr))

name: Optimal Approach
intuition_and_algorithm:
We need to assign a rank to each element of the array based on how small or large it is compared to others. The smallest number gets rank 1, the next smallest gets rank 2, and so on.
Duplicate elements should get the same rank.


 Make a copy of the array to avoid modifying the original. Sort the copied array in ascending order. Use a map or dictionary to assign rank starting from 1, skipping duplicates. Loop through the original array and replace each element with its rank using the map.

complexities.time: O(N log N), We sort the array which takes O(N log N), then loop over the array multiple times all O(N) operations.
complexities.space: O(N), We store an extra hash map for ranks and copy of the array.

code.python:
class Solution:
    # Function to replace elements by their rank in the array
    def replaceWithRank(self, arr):
        # Make a sorted copy of the array
        sorted_arr = sorted(arr)

        # Create a dictionary to hold ranks
        rank_map = {}

        # Initialize rank counter
        rank = 1

        # Assign ranks to unique elements
        for num in sorted_arr:
            if num not in rank_map:
                rank_map[num] = rank
                rank += 1

        # Build the result using rank map
        result = [rank_map[num] for num in arr]
        return result

# Driver code
obj = Solution()
arr = [1, 5, 8, 15, 8, 25, 9]
res = obj.replaceWithRank(arr)
print(*res)
